cards.back: Special files in a Unix-like system that represent a device, allowing programs to interact with hardware devices

cards.front: What is the purpose of a device special file?
cards.back: To provide a way for programs to access and communicate with hardware devices, such as disk drives or printers

cards.front: Types of device special files in Unix
cards.back: Block devices (e.g. /dev/sda), Character devices (e.g. /dev/tty), and Named pipe special files (e.g. /dev/pipe)

cards.front: How are device special files accessed?
cards.back: Using the device number or the device file name, provided by the operating system

cards.front: Example of a device special file
cards.back: /dev/sda, which represents a hard disk drive, allows programs to read and write data to it
